mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 kinase (targets FLT3‑ITD)
additional_notes: Clinically approved for relapsed/refractory FLT3‑mutant AML; active in MOLM‑13 (FLT3‑ITD) models.

mechanism: Selective FLT3 kinase inhibitor
additional_notes: Potent activity in FLT3‑ITD cell lines (e.g., MOLM‑13).

mechanism: Multi‑kinase inhibition including FLT3
additional_notes: Used frontline in FLT3‑mutant AML; nanomolar to sub‑micromolar potency reported.

mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases including FLT3 among others
additional_notes: Has reported activity in FLT3‑ITD AML and off‑label/clinical use in FLT3 cases; potency varies by assay.

mechanism: Selective BCL‑2 inhibition promoting apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved in AML combination regimens; potent in sensitive AML models and often used in combinations.

mechanism: Inhibits BCL‑2 family (BCL‑2 and BCL‑XL)
additional_notes: Also known as ABT‑263; broader BCL family inhibitor with reported AML activity.

mechanism: Inhibits 26S proteasome leading to proteostasis disruption and apoptosis
additional_notes: Studied in AML preclinical studies and combinations; cell‑line dependent potency.

category: Repurposed gold compound / thioredoxin reductase inhibitor
mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase and induces oxidative stress
additional_notes: Repurposing candidate with reported cytotoxicity in AML including FLT3‑mutant lines; potency often in low‑micromolar range.

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial protein translation, impairing mitochondrial function
additional_notes: Reported to preferentially target AML stem/progenitor cells by affecting mitochondria; preclinical evidence.

mechanism: Inhibits CDKs (notably CDK9 and other CDKs) affecting transcription
additional_notes: Also known as Alvocidib; clinical and preclinical activity in AML.

mechanism: Pan‑CDK inhibition leading to cell‑cycle and transcriptional effects
additional_notes: Preclinical AML efficacy reported.

mechanism: Inhibits XPO1/CRM1, blocking nuclear export of tumor suppressors and other cargos
additional_notes: Approved in other hematologic malignancies and under study in AML; activity reported in AML cell lines.

mechanism: Inhibits protein synthesis (translation elongation)
additional_notes: Also called homoharringtonine; used in CML with reported leukemia activity; nanomolar to low‑micromolar potency reported.

mechanism: Induces oxidative stress and can cause proteasome inhibition in cancer cells (often copper‑dependent)
additional_notes: Activity in AML reported in preclinical studies when combined with copper; context‑dependent potency.

mechanism: Inhibits HMG‑CoA reductase affecting cholesterol biosynthesis and prenylation pathways
additional_notes: Epidemiologic/preclinical signals in AML; often tested in combinations; listed generically as statins.

mechanism: Lysosomotropic agent that impairs autophagy and lysosomal function
additional_notes: Also represented by hydroxychloroquine; frequently used as a chemosensitizer in AML preclinical studies.

mechanism: Inhibits HSP90, destabilizing client oncoproteins
additional_notes: Also called Tanespimycin; reported preclinical activity in AML (nanomolar to low‑micromolar).

mechanism: Inhibits mTOR signaling pathway (mTORC1)
additional_notes: Also represented by everolimus; mTOR pathway inhibitors studied in AML with variable dependence.

mechanism: Inhibits SRC/ABL and multiple other kinases affecting signaling
additional_notes: Reported AML cell‑line activity in some contexts and tested clinically in subsets; off‑target kinase profile may be relevant.

mechanism: Inhibits MDM2 to reactivate p53 signaling in TP53‑wildtype cells
additional_notes: Representative of MDM2 inhibitors; activity is genotype‑dependent (TP53 wildtype); nanomolar to low‑micromolar potency reported.
